update.availableTooltip: Version {{version}} is available and downloading in the background
update.restartTooltip: Version {{version}} is ready — restart to apply
update.confirmBody: Updating will close and reinstall the app. Any in-progress agent work or unsaved changes may be lost.
update.confirmHint: If you have important custom agent work, finish it first, then update.

agentStatus.tooltipWaiting: No agents in this project yet
agentStatus.tooltipWorking: {{active}} of {{total}} agents running in this project

guide.title: Guide
guide.subtitle: A tour of what Vibisual can do — and an inventory of features built so far.

start.intro: Vibisual visualizes what your AI agents are doing, in real time, as a bubble map.
start.openFolderD: File → Open Folder to start tracking a project. Each project opens in its own tab.
start.watchD: When Claude Code runs, the files and folders it touches appear as bubbles and pulse as they change.
start.optionsD: File → Options to choose the default model, tools, permission mode and Claude Code binary for new agents.

bubbleMap.intro: The canvas draws your project as bubbles linked by hierarchy and activity.
bubbleMap.typesD: Files, folders, agents, Bash, edits, iframes and more — each color-coded by category.
bubbleMap.sizeD: Bigger bubbles were touched more often. A pulsing bubble is active right now.
bubbleMap.contextD: An agent bubble fills like water to show how much of its context window is in use.
bubbleMap.ghostD: Deleted or renamed nodes linger as faded ghosts so the trail isn't lost.

agents.intro: Beyond passive tracking, you can create and orchestrate your own agents from the canvas.
agents.customD: Right-click the canvas to spawn your own agent bubble and give it a command.
agents.autoD: Describe what you want in plain language; a builder agent designs and wires up a whole team for you.
agents.configD: Click a bubble to set its model, tools, permission mode, effort, isolation and color.
agents.pipelineD: Run independent sessions and chain agents together with pipeline, teams or hybrid strategies.

taskEdges.intro: Connect two custom agents with a Task Edge to delegate work between them.
taskEdges.drawD: Drag from one custom-agent bubble to another to create a delegation edge between them.
taskEdges.kindsD: Command, artifact, request or critique — the kind says what flows across the edge.
taskEdges.critiqueD: A critique edge can force the target agent to rework its output until a reviewer accepts it.

ide.intro: Double-click an agent to open the IDE overlay — its live output, sessions and reports.
ide.streamD: Follow the agent's messages, tool calls and results as they happen.
ide.cardsD: Finished work and things you need to do yourself are split into color-coded cards.
ide.reviewD: When an agent finishes a fix, a purple card shows what changed and what to check.
ide.dockD: Drag the overlay's title bar to float it, or snap it to the right as a side panel.

navigation.panD: Drag the canvas to pan and scroll to zoom; the minimap shows where you are.
navigation.tabsD: Each open folder is a tab; drag a tab out of the bar to pop it into its own window.
navigation.inspectorD: Hold Alt to highlight elements and click to copy; Shift-drag to select a region.
navigation.copyD: Select custom agents and edges, then Ctrl+C / Ctrl+V to clone them into another project.

history.intro: Vibisual remembers your projects between runs.
history.saveD: The whole graph is checkpointed to disk and restored the next time you open Vibisual.
history.replayD: Scrub a timeline to replay how the map grew over a session.

shortcuts.intro: Keyboard shortcuts speed up navigation and editing. They pause while you type in an input or terminal.
shortcuts.bookmarkD: Alt+1…0 pins a bubble or session to a slot; press 1…0 to jump back to it.
shortcuts.copyD: Ctrl+C / Ctrl+V (Cmd on macOS) copies and pastes selected agents, edges and comment boxes.
shortcuts.inspectorD: Hold Alt to inspect and click to copy an element; Shift-drag for region select.
shortcuts.closeD: Esc closes menus, popups and the IDE overlay.

options.title: Options
options.comingSoonDesc: This category is reserved for future settings.

version.title: Version & About
version.intro: The Claude Code binary Vibisual uses to spawn agents, and where it comes from.
version.restartHint: Selection saved. Restart Vibisual to apply it to newly spawned agents.
version.marketplace: Claude Code on VS Code Marketplace

agent.intro: These defaults apply to newly created custom agents. Existing agents are not affected.
agent.rulesPlaceholder:
# Optional default rules injected into every new agent
- ...

autoAgent.title: Auto Agent
autoAgent.description: Harness builder. Describe your goal and a builder agent designs the multi-agent harness for you — choosing the agents, their models, tools and permissions, and wiring the task connections — then kicks it off. You don't lay out bubbles or edges by hand.
autoAgent.placeholder: Describe the goal — e.g. "Add OAuth login to the dashboard and write tests". I'll pick the agents, models and tools and wire them up.
autoAgent.toggleQuestionsTip: When ON, the builder may ask a few clarifying questions before constructing the harness.
autoAgent.clarifying: A couple of clarifications before I build the harness
autoAgent.entryHint: Sub-agents now running. Talk to me here for follow-ups.
autoAgent.buildingHint: The builder is designing agents and connections on the canvas. Open this bubble to watch it work.

phase.idle: Idle — type a request to start

detailPanel.hookAgentSettingsLocked: Hook-registered agents are owned by Claude Code itself. Settings can only be changed for agents created from the canvas.

contiHistory.title: Conti History
contiHistory.generateTitle: Open IDE overlay to send a conti command to the parent agent
contiHistory.empty: No conti yet — press 'Generate new conti' to capture the first take

contiPrompt.title: Patch element with prompt
contiPrompt.placeholder: e.g. change to a circle, recolor to red, edit label...
contiPrompt.hint: Enter to run, Esc to cancel

gitStatus.needsGit: Git is required to start version control
gitStatus.initConfirm:
Create a new git repo in "{{projectName}}"?

git init will create a .git folder in this directory.
gitStatus.syncNote: The worktree was reverted to its original state with merge --abort. Resolve the conflict in your terminal.
gitStatus.commitNote: user.email / user.name may not be configured, or a pre-commit hook rejected the commit.
gitStatus.commitTitleClean: clean working tree — nothing to commit

worktreeDelete.title: Delete worktree
worktreeDelete.mergedInto: Branch {{branch}} is already merged into {{base}}. The worktree folder will be removed.
worktreeDelete.notMerged: Branch {{branch}} has commits not yet in {{base}}. Merge into {{base}} before deleting?
worktreeDelete.branchUnresolved: Could not resolve branch via git worktree list. Deleting anyway will just remove the folder + bubble.

autoEdge.empty: No delegation edges connected. Draw an edge to another custom agent on the canvas and it appears here automatically.
autoEdge.title: Connected edges (auto, read-only)
autoEdge.inactiveWarn: The target has no tools, so this edge is not injected into the source prompt. Add tools to the target to auto-restore it.
autoEdge.footnote: This section is auto-generated from edge state. It is injected into the prompt when the sub-agent runs, so the agent knows whether and how it can delegate.

agentConfig.title: Agent Settings
agentConfig.noRulesDefined: No rules defined — click to edit

model.label: Model
model.opus: Most capable. Best reasoning and complex tasks. Highest cost.
model.sonnet: Balanced performance and cost. Good for most tasks.
model.haiku: Fastest and cheapest. Best for simple read-only tasks.
model.unknown: {{name}} — newly released model. Capabilities and cost inferred from family defaults.

preset.label: Preset
preset.tip: Quick-start: fills tools / permissionMode / effort / rules in one click. Fully editable afterwards (metadata only).
preset.applied: Preset applied — form is freely editable
preset.code-reviewer: Code Reviewer: security/perf/quality, read-only, high effort.

permissionMode.label: Permission Mode
permissionMode.default: Prompts for confirmation on risky actions (writes, shell).
permissionMode.acceptEdits: Auto-approves file edits (Write, Edit). Still prompts for Bash.
permissionMode.plan: Read-only. Cannot modify files or run commands.
permissionMode.bypassPermissions: No prompts. Agent runs without any confirmation.

permissionTimeoutPolicy.label: On no response (60s)
permissionTimeoutPolicy.tip: If the approval popup is not answered within 60s — Allow: auto-approve so the agent keeps working while you are away. Deny: auto-block (safe). Only applies when Permission Mode actually pops a dialog.

disallowedTools.label: Disallowed Tools
disallowedTools.tip: Force-blocked via CLI --disallowedTools. Takes precedence over the allow list (Tools). Use this when you want a single tool off without rebuilding the allow list.

customMode.label: Custom Mode
customMode.tip: Vibisual-only mode axis. Saving Conti mode ON overwrites the agent rules with the conti-only ruleset (previous rules go to history). Review/Debug coming later.
customMode.disabledNote: Coming soon — selection blocked. Saved configuration would be inert.
customMode.placeholderHint: This mode is not implemented yet — saved but inactive.
customMode.contiHint: Saving will spawn a conti bubble next to the agent (single click = history, double click = board).
customMode.contiOverwriteWarn: On save, the agent rules will be overwritten with the conti-only ruleset (previous rules are kept in history).
customMode.contiOffNotice: On save, the conti ruleset is cleared (conti data is preserved). Restore previous rules manually from history.

rulesEditor.help: Custom rules injected into the agent prompt on every run (Markdown)

rulesHistory.title: History
rulesHistory.empty: No history yet — when you change and save rules, the previous value is captured here.
rulesHistory.restoreTitle: Load into the editor (dirty until you save)

isolation.label: Isolation
isolation.none: Works directly in your working directory.
isolation.worktree: Isolated git worktree. Changes won't affect your files.

effort.label: Effort
effort.default: Model's default thinking depth. No override.
effort.low: Minimal thinking. Faster but less thorough.
effort.xhigh: Extra-high reasoning (Opus 4.7+). Recommended for most coding work.
effort.max: Maximum reasoning, no token limit (Opus 4.8). For the hardest judgment calls; may overthink simple tasks.

tools.label: Tools
tools.Read: Read file contents from the filesystem.
tools.Write: Create new files or overwrite existing ones.
tools.Edit: Make targeted string replacements in files.
tools.Bash: Execute shell commands in the terminal.
tools.Grep: Search file contents using regex patterns.
tools.Agent: Spawn sub-agents to delegate complex tasks.
tools.WebFetch: Fetch and read content from URLs.
tools.lockedHint: Required for delegation dispatch (Bash curl) — cannot be removed.

fieldTips.model: The Claude model this agent uses.
fieldTips.permissionMode: Controls what the agent can do without prompting.
fieldTips.rules: Custom instructions injected into the agent prompt on every run (Markdown).
fieldTips.tools: Tools this agent is allowed to use.
fieldTips.maxTurns: Max conversation turns before the agent stops. Prevents runaway loops.
fieldTips.isolation: Whether to run in an isolated git worktree.
fieldTips.skills: Skills auto-loaded on start. Vibisual extension — not standard Claude Code.
fieldTips.color: Custom bubble color in the visualization.
fieldTips.projectContext: Read-only. Files and rules automatically injected into every prompt when this agent runs. Refreshed on open.
fieldTips.agentSettingsNote: These settings apply only when running commands from Vibisual (sub-agent execution). They do not change the agent's own Claude Code session.

permissionPrompt.title: Permission required
permissionPrompt.bodyHint: This agent is about to call a tool. Approve or deny to continue.

claudeVersionGate.title: Claude Code update available
claudeVersionGate.vscodeNotice: This binary comes from the VS Code extension and cannot be auto-updated. Open the Marketplace to update.
claudeVersionGate.unknownNotice: Could not detect a working `claude` binary. Install Claude Code manually before continuing.
claudeVersionGate.cannotAutoInstall: Auto-update unavailable for this install method

iframeServerLog.buttonHint: Opens a popup that live-tails this dev server's stdout/stderr. No data streams until opened.
iframeServerLog.noPort: Port not detected from URL — logs unavailable.
iframeServerLog.title: Server Logs
iframeServerLog.emptyFiltered: No lines match the current filter.

unavailable.generic: This server's logs are not available.
unavailable.no-output-file: Log file path unknown — the process likely wasn't started via a tracked background shell.
unavailable.no-server-entry: No server entry matches this port.

serverLog.title: Server Logs
serverLog.recentOnlyTitle: Render only the latest 200 lines (the buffer keeps the rest)
serverLog.clearLocal: Clear local view (the server keeps its buffer)
serverLog.emptyFiltered: No lines match the current filter.
serverLog.disconnected: Not connected to a server — nothing to show.

debugPanel.title: Debug
debugPanel.restartTimeout: Timeout — server did not restart within 15s. Check the tsx watch process.
debugPanel.restartNoServerInfo: Could not read server info — /api/server-info is not responding

fileEdit.limitHint: Uncheck to store every diff for this file without limit (uses more memory)

taskEdgeDetail.bundleAutoArtifactDesc: This edge is the auto-generated result-return channel for the command edge. Edit or delete from the command edge instead.
taskEdgeDetail.bundleBothDesc: An artifact-return edge was auto-generated because returnFormat=both. Deleting either removes the whole bundle.
taskEdgeDetail.bundleAutoReworkDesc: This edge is the auto-generated rework-command channel for the critique edge. Edit or delete from the critique edge instead.
taskEdgeDetail.bundleForceReworkDesc: A rework-command edge was auto-generated because critique authority=force-rework. Deleting either removes the whole bundle.
taskEdgeDetail.bundleOrphan: Sibling edge not found — only bundleId remains.
taskEdgeDetail.critiqueEscalated: Escalated — downgraded to comment-only after exceeding rework limit.
taskEdgeDetail.autoSiblingReadOnly: Auto-generated edge — read-only. Edit or delete from the primary edge.
taskEdgeDetail.deleteBundleTooltip: Delete the entire bundle (primary + auto sibling)

terminal.unavailable: The embedded terminal is available only in the Vibisual desktop app.
terminal.harnessNote: Interactive session you drive — Vibisual visualizes & assists harness wiring, but execution authority lives inside Claude Code.

mainArea.inputPlaceholderNew: Enter command to start a new session...
mainArea.readOnly: Read-only — select a session tab or create new

askQuestion.title: Question
askQuestion.notePlaceholder: Add a free-form comment to send with your answer.
askQuestion.hint: Pick an option to answer the agent.

streamRenderer.linkFrameBlockedHint: Some sites refuse to be embedded. If it stays blank, open it in your browser.

taskEdgePopup.templateTooltip: Select a preset pair. Picking one auto-fills Type, Gate, and all advanced options below.
taskEdgePopup.templateSelect: Preset role pair. Selecting one prefills the whole form with sensible defaults.
taskEdgePopup.messageTooltip: The instruction sent to the target agent. Press Ctrl/Cmd+Enter to submit.
taskEdgePopup.typeTooltip: What this edge means — Command (instruction), Artifact (deliverable), Request (help ask), Critique (review). Edge color reflects this in the map.
taskEdgePopup.gateTooltip: Auto: runs as soon as saved. Manual: requires a human to press Run. Use Manual for anything destructive or high-impact.
taskEdgePopup.autoTooltip: Auto: executes immediately when the edge is saved. Fastest, but skips human review.
taskEdgePopup.manualTooltip: Manual: waits for a human to press Run. Safer for destructive or high-impact tasks.
taskEdgePopup.advancedTooltip: Message format / Return format / Timeout / Retry / Cache / Priority. Templates prefill these with sensible defaults — usually no change needed. (Execution logic is a future step; this version only stores the values.)
taskEdgePopup.advancedShow: Show advanced options: message format, return format, timeout, retry, cache, priority.
taskEdgePopup.messageFormatTooltip: Free: easy but ambiguous. Schema: key/value, structured. Prefer Schema for critical or repeatable tasks.
taskEdgePopup.freeTextTooltip: Free text: natural-language instruction. Fast to write, but the AI may misread nuance.
taskEdgePopup.schemaTooltip: Schema: structured key/value input. More upfront work, fewer downstream surprises.
taskEdgePopup.schemaEditTooltip: Open the schema editor. Type the format the source agent should follow when sending through this edge.
taskEdgePopup.schemaEditHint: Free-form text. The source agent will be told to format its outbound message in this shape. Leave empty to send as plain text.
taskEdgePopup.schemaPlaceholder:
Example:
{
  "goal": "...",
  "context": "...",
  "acceptance": ["..."]
}

— or natural-language —
Reply with: 1) what you changed, 2) which files, 3) any open risks.
taskEdgePopup.returnFormatTooltip: Artifact: actual file/diff paths. Summary: text summary only. Both: both. AI summaries can be wrong; prefer Artifact for anything you need to verify.
taskEdgePopup.artifactTooltip: Artifact: return the actual file path or diff. Verifiable by you.
taskEdgePopup.summaryTooltip: Summary: short text summary only. Fast, but AI summaries can be inaccurate.
taskEdgePopup.bothTooltip: Both: return the artifact and a summary so you can scan before diving in.
taskEdgePopup.timeoutTooltip: Max run duration in milliseconds. Leave blank for unlimited. Example: 60000 = 1 minute. Guards against runaway loops.
taskEdgePopup.retryTooltip: How many times to retry on failure. 0 = no retries. Useful for transient failures like network hiccups.
taskEdgePopup.retryDisabledNote: Coming soon — retry logic is not implemented yet. Value is saved as metadata only.
taskEdgePopup.priorityTooltip: Run order when multiple edges are ready. High runs first. Default Normal.
taskEdgePopup.priorityDisabledNote: Coming soon — priority scheduling is not implemented yet. Value is saved as metadata only.
taskEdgePopup.priorityLowTooltip: Low: runs only when nothing else is queued.
taskEdgePopup.priorityHighTooltip: High: jumps ahead of other pending edges.
taskEdgePopup.cacheTooltip: Reuse the previous result when inputs match (saves tokens). Turn OFF if the underlying data changes often.
taskEdgePopup.cacheLabelTooltip: When the input matches a previous run, reuse that result instead of re-running. Saves tokens. Turn OFF if underlying data changes often.
taskEdgePopup.cacheDisabledNote: Coming soon — result cache is not implemented yet. Value is saved as metadata only.
taskEdgePopup.delegationTooltip: STRICT: tasks matching this edge's purpose must be delegated through it. The source agent cannot handle them with its own Read/Grep. AUTO: the source agent decides whether to delegate based on cost and fit (3+ exploration steps / purpose match / target suitability). Simple 1-2 Read calls stay in-agent.
taskEdgePopup.delegationStrictTooltip: Mandatory delegation when the edge purpose matches. No self-handling. (Default)
taskEdgePopup.delegationAutoTooltip: The source agent decides autonomously based on exploration cost and fit.
taskEdgePopup.commandOptionsTooltip: Command-edge specifics (kind='command' only). Orthogonal to Delegation Policy: this controls the FORM of delegation, not whether it's enforced.
taskEdgePopup.commandModeTooltip: How tools are shared between parent and child. Shared: parent keeps every tool, can call directly or delegate. Tool delegation: tools the child has are stripped from the parent — delegation enforced naturally. Mode delegation: tools stay shared but the parent is instructed to delegate operations whose mode (plan / acceptEdits / specific model / skill) is the child's specialty.
taskEdgePopup.commandModeSharedTooltip: Parent keeps the tools. Both parent and child can run them. Delegation is recommended via prompt only. (Default — safest.)
taskEdgePopup.commandModeToolTooltip: Tools the child agent has are removed from the parent. The parent must dispatch via this edge to use them. (= Pre-v1.44 strict behavior.)
taskEdgePopup.commandModeModeTooltip: Parent keeps the tools, but the system prompt forces delegation of operations whose mode (plan / acceptEdits / specific model / skill) is the child's specialty. Pseudo-tool injection is a future round.
taskEdgePopup.commandModeModeNote: Mode delegation does not strip any tools — the parent is told via system prompt to delegate operations matching the child's mode. PreToolUse hook gating is planned for a follow-up.
taskEdgePopup.commandStripPreviewTooltip: Live computed: child agent's tools ∩ parent agent's tools. These are the tools the parent will lose access to when this edge is active.
taskEdgePopup.commandStripPreviewEmpty: No overlap — parent and child share no tools. Strip would be a no-op. Consider Mode delegation instead.
taskEdgePopup.cancelTooltip: Discard changes and close. The live preview on the canvas will revert.
taskEdgePopup.saveTooltip: Save these changes to the edge (Ctrl/Cmd+Enter).
taskEdgePopup.connectTooltip: Create the edge with these settings (Ctrl/Cmd+Enter).
taskEdgePopup.critiqueOptionsTip: Critique-edge only. Sets when to intervene (Timing) and what to enforce on reject (Authority). Ignored for other kinds.
taskEdgePopup.critiqueTimingTip: Intermediate (default): review at each milestone during the target's work. More frequent / safer / slower. Final: a single final review after completion. Less frequent / faster.
taskEdgePopup.critiqueTimingIntermediateTip: Intermediate: review at each milestone during the target's work. (default)
taskEdgePopup.critiqueTimingFinalTip: Final only: a single final review after the target completes.
taskEdgePopup.critiqueAuthorityTip: Force rework (default): on reject the target must redo the work. Exceeding Max Rework auto-downgrades to comment-only and escalates to the parent session. Comment only: reject is advisory only, no forced rework.
taskEdgePopup.critiqueAuthorityForceTip: Force rework: the target must redo on reject. (default)
taskEdgePopup.critiqueAuthorityCommentTip: Comment only: the target only takes the reject as a reference. No forced rework.
taskEdgePopup.critiqueMaxReworkTip: Max times the target may redo while in Force rework mode. Exceeding it auto-downgrades to comment-only and escalates to the parent session. A guard against infinite loops.
taskEdgePopup.critiqueMaxReworkActive: Max {{max}} times. Auto-downgrades to comment-only when exceeded.
taskEdgePopup.critiqueMaxReworkInactive: Not used in Comment only mode.

inspector.modeInspector: Inspector — click to copy, wheel: parent

tabMenu.pinTooltip: Pinned tabs are protected from Close Others / Close to the Right / Close All.
tabMenu.defaultTooltip: Default tab opens automatically when there is no last-active tab to restore.

overlay.sendToOverlay: Pop this bubble out as an always-on-top desktop widget
overlay.removeFromOverlay: Remove this bubble from the desktop overlay

tabDetach.detachHint: Drop outside the tab bar to open in a new window
tabDetach.osDragHint: Drag here to move the window
tabDetach.detachCardTitle: Drop here to open as new window
tabDetach.detachCardSubtitle: Release the mouse outside the tab bar
tabDetach.reorderCardSubtitle: Stay inside the tab bar to reorder
tabDetach.redockCardTitle: Drop on the main tab bar to redock
tabDetach.redockCardSubtitle: Or release anywhere else to leave detached
tabDetach.missingProject: The project "{{name}}" is not loaded in this Vibisual instance.

canvas.stubPlaceholder: This project is on disk only. Click the tab to load.

contextMenu.createAutoAgentHint: Meta agent — auto-spawns a custom agent team from your prompt

bounds.label: Bubble Bounds
bounds.tooltip: Rectangular area that keeps bubbles from drifting off-canvas. Drag any edge to resize. Saved per project.

paste.invalidVersion: Clipboard format is incompatible with this version

bookmark.assignEmpty: Nothing to bookmark — select a bubble or open an agent IDE first
bookmark.jumpMissing: Bookmark target is no longer available

notifications.agentCompleted: Agent completed — click to view results

empty.hookInstall: Install hooks with: npx tsx hooks/install.ts
